implementation_core_courses.name: Internet-Based Methods, Extended Reality, and Machine Learning for Use in Engineering Design and Data Analysis
implementation_core_courses.description: Extensive overview of developing and deploying internet-based computational resources for engineering applications. Relational databases, server and client-side web programming, and their use with traditional engineering software (i.e. computer-aided design and finite-element modeling). Extended reality (XR) technologies and machine learning / artificial intelligence for use in internet-based solutions.

faq.question: Do you have to have programming experience to enroll?
faq.answer: No! But this is an Implementation course, so you will learn programming.

faq.question: Is there a prerequisite course I must take?
faq.answer: No prerequisite course is required to enroll.

hci_recommended_electives.name: Special Topics - Managing UX Teams and Delivering Design Systems
hci_recommended_electives.notes: To register, please submit a prerequisite override request.
hci_recommended_electives.details: Courses structure: The course structure focuses on problem solving in the digital transformation space and design systems. The last module and final project deliverable on “Design Automation” allow students to deep dive into those concepts. Project Deliverable Examples: While there will be plenty of creative flexibility in the deliverables to explore concepts that resonate with you, these examples provide guidance on basic requirements. Time management: The low number of group deliverables allows balanced time management throughout the course. Each student will take on the role of “head of product design” for the individual project submissions for the Managing UX section and then work in small groups on the more complex design systems and transformation deliverables. While students are not required to attend class live synchronously, live presentation slots will be available over weekend as well to accommodate schedules if live presentation is desired. Collaboration: To promote learning from peers, there will be an opportunity to reflect on project ideas through peer reviews. In addition, the instructor will share key observations and takeaways to learn from each project submission. Weekly informal conversations: every week there will be a session where the instructor will share real life experiences relevant to the concepts and discussions posts for the week. Students can join the synchronous session to pose their questions real-time or view the recording offline. Coffee with instructor sessions: there will be an opportunity to connect with the instructor to deep dive on industry trends, UX careers, organizational dynamics, change management or any of the course related concepts.

general_electives_suggestions.name: Visual Rhetoric in Professional Communication
general_electives_suggestions.description: Rhetorical theory and research in graphics, document design, and related principles of visual communication. Methods of designing texts, data displays, illustrations, and other visual elements in business and technical communication.
general_electives_suggestions.delivery_mode: in-person; Tuesday and Thursday from 2:10 to 3:25

general_electives_suggestions.name: Workshops: Educational Technology
general_electives_suggestions.delivery_mode: Online; Meet once a month on Tuesdays from 5:30 PM - 7:30 PM CST | Synchronous meetings, 9/2, 10/7, 11/11, 12/9 (The rest are asynchronous)
general_electives_suggestions.description: Practical experience in designing, developing, and evaluating online learning environments for diverse audiences. Explores recent trends, technologies, and pedagogies in online education.
